title: Refactor for Readability
description: Improve clarity of a function without changing its behavior.
content:
Refactor this code for readability without changing its behavior:

```
{{code}}
```

Focus on: clear names, smaller functions, early returns over nesting, and removing incidental complexity. Do NOT change the public interface or observable behavior.

For each change, give a one-line reason. At the end, state how I can verify behavior is unchanged.

title: Extract and Decompose
description: Break a large function or class into well-named, focused units.
content:
This unit is too big and does too much:

```
{{code}}
```

Decompose it. Identify the distinct responsibilities, propose a set of smaller functions/types with clear names and single responsibilities, and show how they compose back into the original behavior. Keep coupling low and avoid leaking internals. Flag any responsibility that probably belongs in a different module entirely.

title: Remove Duplication
description: Find and consolidate duplicated logic safely.
content:
Find the duplication in this code and consolidate it:

```
{{code}}
```

Be careful: only unify code that is the same for the same *reason* (true duplication), not code that merely looks similar today but may diverge. For each consolidation, name the single concept it captures. If some near-duplicates should stay separate, say why.

title: Introduce a Design Pattern
description: Apply an appropriate design pattern to untangle code — or argue against it.
content:
Here is code that feels tangled: 

```
{{code}}
```

Would a design pattern help? If yes, recommend the specific pattern, explain exactly what problem it solves here, and show the refactor. If a pattern would be over-engineering, say so and propose the simpler structural fix instead. Bias toward the simplest thing that removes the pain.

title: Make Code Testable
description: Restructure hard-to-test code to enable unit testing.
content:
This code is hard to test:

```
{{code}}
```

Refactor it to be testable: introduce seams for dependencies (I/O, time, randomness, network), separate decision logic from side effects, and make outputs observable. Don't change behavior. Then show one example test that the new structure makes possible but the old one didn't.

title: Modernize Legacy Code
description: Bring old code up to current language idioms and APIs.
content:
Modernize this legacy {{language}} code to current idioms and APIs:

```
{{code}}
```

Replace deprecated APIs, adopt modern language features where they genuinely improve clarity or safety, and tighten types. Preserve behavior exactly. List each deprecation you removed and what replaced it. Do not modernize for its own sake — skip changes that add churn without benefit.

title: Reduce Cyclomatic Complexity
description: Flatten deeply nested or branch-heavy logic.
content:
This function has too many branches/nesting levels:

```
{{code}}
```

Reduce its complexity using guard clauses, early returns, lookup tables, polymorphism, or extraction — whichever fits. Keep behavior identical, including edge cases. Show the before/after complexity intuition and confirm every original branch is still covered.

title: Tidy First, Then Change
description: Separate a structural cleanup from a behavioral change in two steps.

title: Replace Conditional with Polymorphism
description: Turn a sprawling type-switch into polymorphic dispatch, or leave it.

title: Untangle a Circular Dependency
content:
These modules depend on each other circularly: {{modules}}

Diagnose the cycle and break it. Options: extract the shared piece into a third module, invert a dependency via an interface or callback, or merge them if they are really one concept. Recommend the cleanest for my case and show the new dependency direction. Explain what the cycle was hiding about the design.

variables.name: modules
variables.description: The modules and how they depend on each other

title: Rename for Clarity
description: Propose better names for confusing identifiers.

title: Simplify a Boolean Expression
description: Make complex conditional logic readable and correct.
